evals.prompt: Read the spec at evals/files/spec-parallelizable.md. Don't interview me — the spec is already complete. Just add the Work Units section to the end of it. Write the result to outputs/spec-parallelizable-with-units.md
evals.expected_output: A spec with Work Units section that identifies 3 parallel scraper units and a foundation unit for requirements.txt + shared utilities. Execution strategy should be 'Foundation → Parallel'. The notebook should be sequential (depends on scraper output) OR part of post-merge verification.
expectations: Output contains a '## Work Units' section
Execution strategy is NOT 'Sequential' — the 3 scrapers are independent
The 3 scraper scripts are assigned to separate parallel units
No two parallel units share the same file
requirements.txt is in the foundation unit, not a parallel unit
Runtime dependencies section exists and addresses the notebook's dependency on the scrapers' CSV output
A post-merge verification section exists with an E2E test recipe

evals.prompt: Read the spec at evals/files/spec-sequential.md. Don't interview me — the spec is already complete. Just add the Work Units section to the end of it. Write the result to outputs/spec-sequential-with-units.md
evals.expected_output: A spec with Work Units section that correctly identifies the notebook imports classes from quant_models.py — a runtime dependency that prevents parallel execution. Execution strategy should be 'Sequential'.
expectations: Output contains a '## Work Units' section
Execution strategy is 'Sequential' (single worker) — NOT 'Foundation → Parallel'
The runtime/import dependency is explicitly identified: notebook imports PublicOwnership and EVOptimizedSimulator from quant_models.py
Does NOT recommend parallel worktrees for these two units
Dependency analysis section mentions that the notebook cannot run without the classes existing first

evals.prompt: Read the spec at evals/files/spec-mixed.md. Don't interview me — the spec is already complete. Just add the Work Units section to the end of it. Write the result to outputs/spec-mixed-with-units.md
evals.expected_output: A spec with Work Units section that identifies foundation work (scaffold, shared types, DB schema, auth middleware), parallel frontend components, and notes that backend routes depend on auth middleware + types (so they should be foundation or sequential). Execution strategy should be 'Foundation → Mixed' or 'Foundation → Parallel' depending on how foundation is scoped.
expectations: Output contains a '## Work Units' section
Project scaffold and shared types are in the foundation unit
Database schema (Prisma) is in the foundation unit
Auth middleware is either in foundation or a sequential unit that runs before routes
Frontend components that don't depend on each other are in parallel units
Backend routes are NOT in parallel units alongside frontend components if they depend on auth middleware that hasn't been built yet
The api.ts client (which imports shared types) has its dependency on types/ noted
Dependency analysis identifies that backend routes import from types/ and use auth middleware
